title: Map Weekend Labels to Absolute Pirelli Compounds
summary: SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD are relative weekend labels; Pirelli nominates three slick compounds from a wider C-range, so a weekend HARD can be C1, C2, or C3 with different durability.
rationale: SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD are relative weekend labels; Pirelli nominates three slick compounds from a wider C-range, so a weekend HARD can be C1, C2, or C3 with different durability. The current best solutions use global soft/medium/hard life constants, which can blur race-specific tyre windows.
implementation_hint: Add a small Race_Year-to-absolute-compound table mapping each weekend's HARD/MEDIUM/SOFT to C-index. Create AbsoluteCompoundIndex, AbsoluteCompoundLife, softness gap to current weekend hard/soft, and interactions with TyreLife, LapsRemaining_Est, Stint, and Race.
expected_effect: Should improve ranking near pit windows where nominal labels are misleading, especially races using C1-C3 versus C3-C5 selections.
risk: Manual mapping may be incomplete for synthetic or pre-season rows; wrong mappings can hurt more than coarse nominal labels.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
